codeReview.summary: The implementation correctly fixes the no_show trigger by adding a cron-based function that detects confirmed reservations past their scheduled time (2+ hours), marks them as no_show, and sends the reservation.no_show event to trigger automations. The code follows existing patterns in the codebase and passes TypeScript and lint checks.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation follows the existing patterns in the codebase for Inngest functions - cron-based triggers, event sending, and proper error handling with try/catch per item.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The code uses the same patterns as other cron functions like checkLowStock, checkBirthdayTriggers, etc.

summary: Feature m5-3-fix-no-show-trigger correctly implements a cron-based solution to detect no-show reservations. The checkNoShowReservations function runs hourly (cron: 0 * * * *), finds confirmed reservations 2+ hours past their scheduled time, marks them as no_show, and sends the reservation.no_show event. The existing handleNoShowTrigger listens for this event and will fire correctly. TypeScript and lint checks pass for all changed files.
